title: [FIRRTL][ExportVerilog] Mechanism to avoid bound instances from tripping unused wire lint warnings
body:
Currently, if a bound instance is removed, this _correctly_ leaves dangling references (possibly wires) in the original module. This then creates a situation where lint tools complain about unused wires (even though they will be used in a bound context).

We should have a mechanism to work around this, e.g., by generating lint waivers inline in the code or in a separate file. Alternatively, this indicates that FIRRTL's extract test code needs to be more aggressive to extract more.

Consider:
```scala
circuit Extract:
  module Extract:
    input clock: Clock
    input reset: UInt<1>
    input a: UInt<1>

    node en = a

    assert(clock, en, reset, "hello world")
```

When compiling this to Verilog using `firtool Extract.fir --extract-test-code --strip-debug-info`, this produces:
```verilog
module Extract_assert(
  input a,
        reset,
        clock);

  always @(posedge clock) begin
    if (reset)
      assert(a) else $error("hello world");
  end // always @(posedge)
endmodule

module Extract(
  input clock,
        reset,
        a);

  wire en;

  assign en = a;
  /* This instance is elsewhere emitted as a bind statement.
    Extract_assert InvisibleBind_assert (
      .a     (en),
      .reset (reset),
      .clock (clock)
    );
  */
endmodule


// ----- 8< ----- FILE "bindfile" ----- 8< -----

bind Extract Extract_assert InvisibleBind_assert (
  .a     (en),
  .reset (reset),
  .clock (clock)
);
```

Here, `wire en` is dead. Lint tools which are not run with the bindfile (which is a reasonable option given the fact that you want your synthesizable code to be lint clean), will be upset that `wire en` is not used.

This one is potentially complicated to sort through, but it is causing a very large number of lint warnings for us.
